flashcards.back:
¬ NOT (negation)
∧ AND (conjunction)
∨ OR (disjunction)
→ IMPLIES (implication)
↔ IFF (biconditional)
⊕ XOR (exclusive or)

T → T = T
T → F = F ← Only FALSE case!
F → T = T
F → F = T

'True implies False' is the only lie

Given: p → q
Contrapositive: ¬q → ¬p

IMPORTANT:
p → q ≡ ¬q → ¬p (equivalent!)

Converse (q → p) is NOT equivalent
